why_we_would: P2 carries 0.87 of our belief mass, and their unprompted Seer claim on Day 1 is exactly the kind of high-variance cover story a wolf reaches for when accused. Acting on Day 1 matters because hesitation hands the wolf a free night kill; with belief this concentrated, the vote converts our best information into an immediate 87% chance of a clean, zero-casualty win. The simulation confirms the expected value of 0.906 is driven almost entirely by the dominant branch resolving perfectly.
risks: If P2 really is the Seer (the combined 13% where the wolf is P1, P3, or P4), we execute our only information role and the wolf gets a night kill, winning outright in the P1 and P4 worlds. We are also anchoring the village on a single loud claim-versus-dossier judgment with no corroborating check.
